IsA: It is a board wargame of aerial combat.
It is a tactical jet-combat game.
HasFeatureOf: It is characterized by "not a game for beginners."
It is characterized by its clever mechanics.
It is characterized by its amateurish quality of the components.
It is characterized by poorly die-cut counters that were all the same color.
It is characterized by its "beer and pretzels" change of pace.
UsedFor: It is used for simulating combat between jet aircraft from 1945 to 1977.
It is used for aerial combat for two players.
It is used for tracking maneuvers, weaponry and damage.
It is used for dictating how quickly an aircraft can turn in the Basic game.
It is used for limiting each aircraft by the number of G-forces the aircraft frame can take in the Advanced game.
It is used for capturing the essence of plane-to-plane combat in the modern era.
InventedBy: It was invented by Michael Scott Kurtick.
It was invented by Rockland Russo.
PublishedIn: It was published in 1977 by Gamescience.
It was published in the same year as the MiG Killers Expansion Kit.
Affects: It affects the speed of the aircraft coming out of a turn.
It affects whether an aircraft's speed falls below its stall speed.
ConsistsOf: It consists of each player controlling a jet fighter.
It consists of larger aircraft data cards in the expansion kit.
It consists of counters out of another game.
PartOf: It is part of the board wargame genre.
It is part of the aerial combat simulation genre.
It is part of the tactical jet-combat game category.
KnownFor: It is known for its G-force rule.
It is known for its clever mechanics capturing the essence of plane-to-plane combat.
It is known for being "far more playable" than Air War.
It is known for being "probably the best single air warfare game available."
It is known for being "much more advanced than any competitors and also much easier to play."
It is known for being a "promising game system that could have done quite well with some additional rules polishing and an improved presentation."
It is known for being mostly a collector's item.
AssociatedWith: It is associated with Gamescience.
It is associated with Jon Freeman.
It is associated with David Munch.
It is associated with J.D. Webster.
It is associated with Michael Scott Kurtick.
It is associated with Rockland Russo.
It is associated with Simulations Publications Inc. (SPI).
It is associated with the year 1977.
It is associated with the MiG Killers Expansion Kit.
It is associated with Air Strike.
About: It is about combat between jet aircraft from 1945 to 1977.
It is about aerial combat for two players.
It is about fighter aircraft equipped with technology representative of a particular era.
It is about the G-forces an aircraft frame and pilot can take.
It is about the speed of the aircraft coming out of a turn.
It is about the stall speed of an aircraft.
It is about missile combat systems.
It is about radar lock-ons.
It is about gun combat.
It is about tail-chasing IR missiles.
InfluencedBy: It was influenced by modern air combat.
It was influenced by the limitations of pilots and planes.
It was influenced by the need to track variables.
It was influenced by optional and advanced rules.
It was influenced by the much more complex Air War released by Simulations Publications Inc. (SPI).
It was influenced by the capabilities of fighter aircraft.
It was influenced by the technology of a particular era.
It was influenced by the G-force rule.
It was influenced by the missile combat system.
It was influenced by the bookkeeping required.
Influenced: It influenced the way aerial combat games are designed.
It influenced the perception of air warfare games.
It influenced the market for board games.
It influenced the development of future wargames.
It influenced the understanding of tactical jet-combat.
